evidence: In February 2025, we committed to a multi-year project ('Project Nova') to modernize our 'Order to Cash' business processes, which includes the related information technology infrastructure and underlying enabling technologies.
we announced a collaboration with Google to use Google Cloud's data analytics and generative AI to help us streamline our data management, improve data analytics
We have implemented a formal AI governance oversight committee and established multiple AI safeguards to minimize risks associated with AI
In 2025, we initiated or expanded our use of AI and automation in several areas, ranging from digital cytology, microbiology, parasitology and digital pathology.
we advanced our use of automation in front-end specimen processing and labeling in six of our labs, freeing more of our processors to focus on higher-order work.
Quest Lab Stewardship employs machine learning to help optimize medically-appropriate laboratory test utilization.
AI technology can create accuracy issues and other outcomes that could harm our customers and negatively impact our reputation and our business.

Using AI successfully will require significant resources, including having the technical expertise required to develop, test and maintain AI-based testing services and continually developing the appropriate governance and oversight

many of the third-party service providers we rely on use AI for a variety of purposes, which increases the risk that our sensitive and proprietary data...could be inadvertently or maliciously exposed.

limitations: No quantified dollar amounts or GPU/data-center scale figures tied specifically to AI investment were disclosed in the excerpts; Project Nova and Google Cloud collaboration are named but unquantified for AI spend specifically.

Narrative concreteness scored 4 (deployed use cases with operating detail) but no quantified business results (e.g., % productivity gain, cost savings attributable to AI) were provided, preventing a score of 5.

Risk disclosure scored 4 based on named company-specific risk mechanisms (accuracy issues, regulatory requirements, third-party AI exposure, competitive risk); no quantified risk figures or formally named risk-quantification program were disclosed.
